title: Identifying sides and writing ratios
problem: A right-angled triangle has angle $\theta$ at one vertex. The side opposite $\theta$ is $6$ cm, the side adjacent to $\theta$ is $8$ cm, and the hypotenuse is $10$ cm. Write the values of $\sin \theta$, $\cos \theta$ and $\tan \theta$.

steps.instruction: Identify the three sides relative to $\theta$.

title: Evaluating a trig ratio with a calculator
problem: Use a calculator to find $\tan 57°$ correct to 4 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Check that your calculator is set to degree mode.
Round to 4 decimal places. The fifth decimal digit is $6$, so round up.

title: Setting up a trig equation for an unknown side
problem: A right-angled triangle has angle $\theta = 42°$ at one vertex and a hypotenuse of $15$ cm. Write an equation for the length of the opposite side $x$, then calculate $x$ correct to 2 decimal places.

steps.instruction: Identify the known information and the unknown. We know the hypotenuse and the angle, and we want the opposite side.
Choose the ratio that links the opposite side and the hypotenuse. That is SOH: $\sin \theta = \dfrac{\text{opposite}}{\text{hypotenuse}}$.
